summary: Provision new DRS (Elastic Disaster Recovery) replication configuration templates. Every stand-up routes to an engineer for review — source-server association and day-2 operations land in a later wave.

operations.title: Provision a new DRS replication configuration template
operations.summary: Request a brand-new DRS replication settings template; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a new replication configuration template as a complete specification: staging area network placement, replication server sizing, encryption, and a point-in-time snapshot policy. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: template_name
params.label: Template name
params.help: Lower-case letters, numbers and dashes. Names this template in the generated draft — a replication configuration template has no name field of its own.

params.name: staging_area_subnet_id
params.label: Staging area subnet
params.help: Where DRS launches replication servers and staging-area volumes.

params.name: replication_servers_security_groups
params.label: Replication server security groups
params.help: Security groups attached to the replication servers DRS launches.

params.name: associate_default_security_group
params.label: Also attach the VPC default security group
params.help: Off keeps replication traffic scoped to only the security groups picked above.

params.name: create_public_ip
params.label: Assign a public IP
params.help: Off — replication servers stay private, matching the estate's no-public-IP convention.

params.name: data_plane_routing
params.label: Data plane routing
params.help: PRIVATE_IP routes replicated data over the private network — the estate default.

params.name: replication_server_instance_type
params.label: Replication server instance type
params.help: The EC2 instance type DRS launches replication servers as.

params.name: use_dedicated_replication_server
params.label: Use a dedicated replication server
params.help: On dedicates one replication server per source server instead of sharing one — higher cost, isolated throughput.

params.name: bandwidth_throttling
params.label: Bandwidth throttling (Mbps)
params.help: Caps outbound replication traffic per server, in Mbps. 0 means unthrottled.

params.name: default_large_staging_disk_type
params.label: Staging disk type
params.help: The EBS volume type used for large staging-area disks.

params.name: ebs_encryption
params.label: EBS encryption
params.help: CUSTOM lets you pick a customer-managed key below; DEFAULT uses the AWS-owned key.

params.name: ebs_encryption_key
params.label: Encryption key
params.help: Required when EBS encryption is CUSTOM; leave blank for DEFAULT.

params.name: staging_area_name_tag
params.label: Staging area Name tag
params.help: Applied as the Name tag on every staging-area resource DRS creates (volumes, snapshots).

params.name: auto_replicate_new_disks
params.label: Auto-replicate new disks
params.help: Automatically starts replicating a disk added to a source server after it is already protected.

params.name: pit_interval
params.label: Snapshot interval (hours)
params.help: How often point-in-time snapshots are taken.

params.name: pit_units
params.label: Snapshot interval unit
params.help: Hour-based cadence for this starter rule — written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: pit_retention_duration
params.label: Retention (hours)
params.help: How many hours of point-in-time history to retain before the oldest snapshot is dropped.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this replication template does
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this replication template.

decisions: Additional point-in-time policy tiers (e.g. a coarser daily rule on top of the hourly one authored here) are an engineer follow-up
Associating this template with specific source servers happens after the template exists
A launch configuration template (right-sizing, licensing, tags for the recovered instance) is a separate, later engineer step
